title: Pfizer Q4 and Full-Year 2025 Earnings Highlights
text: Pfizer reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results and reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance. Full-year 2025 revenue was $62.6 billion, down 2% operationally from 2024, but revenue excluding Comirnaty and Paxlovid grew 6% operationally. Full-year reported diluted EPS was $1.36 and adjusted diluted EPS was $3.22. Fourth-quarter 2025 revenue was $17.6 billion, down 3% operationally, while revenue excluding Comirnaty and Paxlovid grew 9% operationally. Fourth-quarter reported diluted loss per share was $0.29 and adjusted diluted EPS was $0.66. Growth in the quarter was supported by products such as Abrysvo, oncology biosimilars, Eliquis, Prevnar, Vyndaqel, Lorbrena, and Padcev, but COVID-19 products declined sharply, with Comirnaty down 35% operationally and Paxlovid down 70% operationally. Pfizer reaffirmed 2026 revenue guidance of $59.5 billion to $62.5 billion and adjusted diluted EPS guidance of $2.80 to $3.00. The release is useful RAG evidence because it shows Pfizer's non-COVID business growth, cost discipline, pipeline focus, and continuing pressure from COVID product declines and loss-of-exclusivity risk.
